_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Apply concepts, procedures, software, equipment, and/or technology applications to students.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of adult learning design principles, including learning objectives, instructional design methods, knowledge retention research, and blended learning delivery models.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI OP domain. OP-K-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AB-S-002 (AB, cosine: 0.392), OP-S-004 (OP, cosine: 0.384).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.2/10 (raw 0.323). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [applications, apply, concepts, equipment, procedures, software] vs WIDAI-only terms [adult, blended, delivery, design, instructional, knowledge] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.861 (P=0.867, R=0.855). Balanced P/R — the 10-word DCWF element and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.3%). "Apply concepts, procedures, software, equipment, and/or technology applications" and "Knowledge of adult learning design principles, including learning objectives, in" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.437 > STS 0.323 (gap +0.114). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 3.9%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [applications, apply, concepts, equipment, procedures, software]. WIDAI terms: [adult, blended, delivery, design, instructional, knowledge].
